items.title: Jewish ANU student called 'baby killer' on campus, royal commission hears

children.content: The Royal Commission into Anti Semitism and Social Cohesion has heard universities need to improve responses when Jewish staff and students feel unsafe.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair in a dark blazer and white shirt stands behind a microphone looking off camera.

items.title: ACT signs deal for CSIRO site, plans to build 3,000 homes

children.content: After being flagged for development over a decade ago, the ACT government has reached an agreement for the sale of land at a former CSIRO agriculture research site to construction of thousands new homes.

image.altText: A drone shot of green pastures

items.title: As the mercury drops in Canberra, cyclists are still braving the chill

children.content: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

gallery.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

thumbnail.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

items.title: 'He gave us 10 extra years': Australian-first immune centre opens in Canberra

children.content: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

image.altText: An elderly man and his adult daughter smiling outside.

items.title: First Indigenous Military Cross recipient discovered by War Memorial

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

items.title: Aboriginal artist recreating 'oldest living weaving practice' with glass

children.content: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

gallery.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.
A woman with short grey hair stands in a studio surrounded by glass artworks with woven patterns of colour.
A woman with short grey hair stands in a studio looking at the weaving of an handmade bag.

thumbnail.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.
A woman with short grey hair stands in a studio surrounded by glass artworks with woven patterns of colour.
A woman with short grey hair stands in a studio looking at the weaving of an handmade bag.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: US announces new Iran strikes after attacks reported in Kuwait, Bahrain

children.content: Fresh attacks have rocked Iran and Kuwait as Tehran and Washington square off over the Strait of Hormuz, offering contradicting statements as to whether the vital waterway was open to traffic.

image.altText: A projectile being fired from a ship against Iran.

items.title: Sea World inquest hears crash could have been avoided if pilot looked right

children.content: The chief executive of Sea World Helicopters told a Brisbane inquest he believed pilot Ashley Jenkinson was "totally distracted" by the front row passengers on the joy flight.

image.altText: Two bystanders look on at helicopter crash scene.

items.title: 'Affordable' homes sit vacant more than seven months after completion

children.content: Governments across Australia are spending billions to boost the number of rentals available to people on low and middle incomes. A Four Corners investigation found many of these homes were unaffordable for those who needed them most, particularly single-income households.

gallery.altText: A man with a beard sits in an apartment. His facial expression is serious.

thumbnail.altText: A man with a beard sits in an apartment. His facial expression is serious.

items.title: Jana Armstrong's former partner remanded in custody on murder charge

children.content: Toowoomba man Dharminder Singh, 48, whose former partner, Jana Armstrong's body was discovered on the weekend, has appeared in a Darling Downs court charged with murder.

image.altText: A woman lovingly holds a small baby

items.title: How a young doctor ended up being charged with manslaughter after street scuffle

children.content: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a late-night Fremantle scuffle. What do we know?

image.altText: Mr Joseph wearing a graduating gown and holding frame

items.title: Qld's multi-million-dollar family violence prevention cuts 'indefensible'

children.content: The Queensland opposition has labelled the cuts to family and domestic violence funding "indefensible".

image.altText: Two women speaking to each other.

items.title: UN alleges Hamas stormed Gaza food depot and disrupted aid delivery

children.content: The UN says the event occurred in the north of the war-ravaged strip, with a senior official saying staff were "endangered."

image.altText: A man kneels between two trucks carrying aid.

items.title: Five chicken dinners for cosy nights at home

children.content: From pot pie to tray bakes and sticky maple wings, here are five of our most popular chicken recipes.

thumbnail.altText: A home-cooked chicken pie being served onto two plates with a knife and serving spoon

items.title: Pay deal rejection sparks A-League men's and women's uncertainty

children.content: A-Leagues' athletes and the competition's bosses are at loggerheads over a new pay deal after players voted against the latest proposal.

image.altText: Auckland FC players celebrate with a banner saying 'A-League Champions 2026'.

items.title: New exhibition challenges visitors to slow down and put their phones away

children.content: A new show at Hawthorn Arts Centre offers a range of immersive artworks made from books and paper that demand, and reward, our full attention.

image.altText: Palomita Soledad by Nadia Hernandez featuring large cutout letters adhered to a large canvas, hanging in a gallery.

items.title: 'Devoted' Melbourne mother remembered by family after stabbing death

children.content: Lavanya Chappa has been remembered as a devoted mother and dear friend, one week after the mother-of-two was stabbed to death in her Melbourne home.

items.title: How setting small, specific goals can help reduce your drinking

children.content: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

image.altText: A group of young people drinking and laughing

items.title: How Lindsey Graham's death could affect Donald Trump's agenda

children.content: US senator Lindsey Graham died suddenly on Saturday night, local time, creating an unexpected vacancy in Congress. Here's what we know about what comes next.

image.altText: Lindsey Graham talks in front of a gold-domed tower of Kyiv's Saint Sophia Cathedral.

items.title: 'He continues to inspire us': Richard Scolyer's family pay emotional tribute

children.content: Thousands have gathered for Richard Scolyer's state memorial service, with the leading melanoma pathologist remembered as an esteemed researcher and family man.

articles.title: Tour de Cure reflects on Scolyer's legacy ahead of memorial service

children.content: As Mark Beretta rode his bike up a steep Tasmanian mountain in March with all his might, it was none other than Richard Scolyer who overtook him.

image.altText: Mark Beretta and Richard Scolyer in black lyra, smiling as they stand near a paddock in Tasmania.

gallery.altText: Portrait head and shoulders Richard Scolyer slight smile wearing a suit

thumbnail.altText: Portrait head and shoulders Richard Scolyer slight smile wearing a suit

items.title: Australian woman was cunning 'Sharia judge' in Syrian camp, ABC told

children.content: Speaking exclusively to the ABC, a security source who dealt directly with Australian woman Hodan Abby calls her a driving force behind many of a Syrian detention camp's problems.

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

items.title: Coroner calls for change after man died while paramedic waited outside

children.content: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

image.altText: A green and yellow ambulance driving on a road

items.title: Bazball: An uncomplicated idea leaving a very complex legacy

children.content: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

items.title: 'Psychopath' Tuchel gives England a chance to prove its mettle

children.content: This England World Cup squad is proving they are different to their predecessors at every stop. Now their manager has just given them their latest challenge.

image.altText: Thomas Tuchel speaks to Jude Bellingham

items.title: Sentencing of Molly Ticehurst's killer brought forward

children.content: The sentencing of the man who murdered New South Wales woman Molly Ticehurst has been brought forward to allow a psychiatrist to give evidence.

image.altText: A woman smiles at the camera.

items.title: Sinner triumphs over Zverev for second consecutive Wimbledon win

children.content: The top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev for his second consecutive title at Wimbledon after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue.

articles.title: Hewitt drops Wimbledon boys' title after epic battle against US star

children.content: Cruz Hewitt comes up short in his bid to win the Wimbledon boys' title, being beaten by Jordan Lee in the final.

image.altText: Cruz Hewitt looking frustrated while holding his racquet

articles.title: Nosková downs 'ex-friend' Muchová to win all-Czech Wimbledon final

children.content: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.
